things.name: Newgrange & Brú na Bóinne (UNESCO)
things.description: The world's most celebrated passage tomb — 5,200 years old, aligned with the winter solstice sunrise, surrounded by 90+ monuments

things.name: Hill of Tara (Seat of High Kings)
things.description: The ancient ritual and burial landscape where the High Kings of Ireland were inaugurated — a monument-studded hilltop with legendary status

things.name: Trim Castle (Largest Anglo-Norman Castle)
things.description: Ireland's largest Anglo-Norman castle (1173) with 15 towers — filming location for Braveheart — set beside the River Boyne

things.name: Navan Racecourse
things.description: One of Ireland's finest National Hunt racecourses — home of the Irish Grand National and major fixtures throughout the year

things.name: Hill of Slane
things.description: The hill where St Patrick lit the Paschal Fire in 433 AD defying the High King — ruined friary and superb views over the Boyne valley

things.name: Kells (Market Cross & Book of Kells Link)
things.description: Monastic town where the Book of Kells may have been illuminated — a round tower, four high crosses and a medieval market cross in the town square

things.name: Slane Castle & Village
things.description: Gothic Revival castle in the beautiful Boyne valley — famous for hosting U2, Madonna and other megaconcerts on the sweeping lawns

things.name: Bective Abbey (Cistercian Ruins)
things.description: Atmospheric 12th-century Cistercian abbey ruins on the River Boyne — filming location for Braveheart with beautiful cloisters

things.name: Loughcrew Megalithic Centre (Day Trip)
things.description: Hilltop complex of 30 passage tombs from 3500 BC with spectacular solar alignments — rarely visited, outstanding views across Ireland

things.name: Causey Farm Heritage Experience
things.description: Award-winning authentic Irish farm experience with turf-cutting, thatch weaving, storytelling and an Irish céilí — a favourite with visitors
